title: ChFS — Distributed Filesystem (Labs 1–3)
summary: ChFS is an educational inode-based distributed filesystem built across three sequential labs. Lab 1 builds a single-machine storage stack (block device → inode layer → filesystem layer). Lab 2 lifts that stack into a distributed system (data server, metadata server, RPC client). Lab 3 implements the Raft consensus algorithm independently, sharing only the BlockManager for log persistence.
instructionPreview: You are implementing **ChFS**, an educational distributed filesystem from an undergraduate systems course. The work is organized as **three sequential labs** in one codebase under `/workspace`.
instruction:
You are implementing **ChFS**, an educational distributed filesystem from an undergraduate systems course. The work is organized as **three sequential labs** in one codebase under `/workspace`.

The checked-in starter tree is the **student skeleton**: many APIs end in `UNIMPLEMENTED()` or `// TODO` and must be completed. The reference solution is not present until you implement it yourself (the benchmark oracle applies a private gold patch for grading only).

**Lab 1 — Block device layer**
Complete the block allocator and block manager so basic storage primitives work. Relevant implementation files include `src/block/allocator.cc`, `src/block/manager.cc`, and headers under `src/include/block/`.

**Lab 2 — Metadata, local filesystem, and RPC**
Implement inode/metadata management and local filesystem operations (create, getattr, directory operations, indirect blocks, remove, etc.), then the `librpc` client/server used for remote calls. Key areas: `src/metadata/`, `src/filesystem/`, `src/librpc/`, and matching headers under `src/include/`.

**Lab 3 — Distributed filesystem and Raft**
Implement distributed metadata and data servers, the distributed client, supporting utilities, and the Raft-based replicated state machine (`src/distributed/`, `src/include/distributed/`, `src/include/rsm/raft/`, and related sources).

Build with CMake **from a `build/` subdirectory** (as enforced by the root `CMakeLists.txt`). Use the project-recommended **Clang 14** toolchain (`clang-14` / `clang++-14`). Example:

`mkdir -p build && cd build && cmake .. -DCMAKE_BUILD_TYPE=Release -DCMAKE_C_COMPILER=clang-14 -DCMAKE_CXX_COMPILER=clang++-14 && cmake --build . -j$(nproc)`

moduleDag.description: ChFS is an educational inode-based distributed filesystem built across three sequential labs. Lab 1 builds a single-machine storage stack (block device → inode layer → filesystem layer). Lab 2 lifts that stack into a distributed system (data server, metadata server, RPC client). Lab 3 implements the Raft consensus algorithm independently, sharing only the BlockManager for log persistence.

nodes.label: Block Manager
nodes.description: Implements the raw block device abstraction: write_block, write_partial_block, zero_block, and read_block over an mmap-backed file (or in-memory buffer). All higher layers consume these four primitives; this is the foundation of the entire storage stack.

nodes.label: Raft Leader Election
nodes.description: Defines RequestVote and AppendEntries (heartbeat-only) message types in protocol.h and implements request_vote, handle_request_vote_reply, run_background_election, append_entries (heartbeat), and run_background_ping in node.h. Establishes the leader election and heartbeat mechanism required by all later Raft parts.

nodes.label: Block Allocator
nodes.description: Manages free/allocated blocks via a bitmap stored in dedicated bitmap blocks on the block device. Provides allocate (find first-free bit and set it) and deallocate (clear the bit) using BlockManager I/O. Prerequisite for any layer that needs dynamic block allocation.

nodes.label: Raft Log Replication
nodes.description: Extends AppendEntries to carry actual log entries; implements new_command, run_background_commit (replicate entries to followers and advance commitIndex), and run_background_apply (apply committed entries to the state machine). Depends on the election/heartbeat infrastructure from raft_election.

nodes.label: Inode Manager
nodes.description: Maintains the inode table (inode_id → block_id indirection), inode allocation bitmap, and superblock. Implements allocate_inode, free_inode, get (look up block_id for an inode), and set_table on top of BlockManager and BlockAllocator.

nodes.label: Distributed Data Server
nodes.description: RPC server that stores raw file blocks on behalf of the metadata server and clients. Implements alloc_block/free_block (with version increment to invalidate stale mappings), read_data (with version check), and write_data. Uses BlockAllocator directly; does not require the inode or filesystem layers.

nodes.label: Raft Log Persistence
nodes.description: Implements RaftLog<Command> to persist Raft durable state (current term, voted-for, and log entries) to disk via BlockManager. Loaded in the RaftNode constructor to restore state after a crash, enabling crash-recovery tests.

nodes.label: Filesystem Data Operations
nodes.description: Provides alloc_inode (allocates a block and wraps InodeManager), read_file, and write_file with dynamic direct/indirect block management. These are the core file I/O primitives consumed by the directory layer and the distributed metadata server.

nodes.label: Raft Snapshot
nodes.description: Implements save_snapshot (capture state machine snapshot and trim log) and install_snapshot RPC (send full snapshot to lagging followers). Requires the persistent log from raft_log_persistence and modifies all log-index arithmetic to account for the snapshot's last_included_index offset.

nodes.label: Filesystem Directory Operations
nodes.description: Implements parse_directory, read_directory, append_to_directory, and rm_from_directory (Part 3C) then lookup, mk_helper, and unlink (Part 3D), which compose file I/O with directory-entry serialization to support a full namespace hierarchy.

nodes.label: Distributed Metadata Server
nodes.description: RPC server managing all filesystem metadata: mknode, unlink, lookup, readdir, get_type_attr, allocate_block, free_block, and get_block_map. Reuses the single-node inode/filesystem layer internally and guards all handlers with a global recursive mutex for before-or-after atomicity of concurrent operations.

nodes.label: Distributed Filesystem Client
nodes.description: Client-side implementation: issues metadata RPCs to the metadata server and data RPCs directly to data servers for mknode, unlink, lookup, readdir, read_file, write_file, and free_file_block. Coordinates block-map retrieval, block allocation, and version-based stale-read detection.

edges.label: BlockAllocator reads and writes bitmap blocks using BlockManager I/O primitives
InodeManager reads/writes inode-table and bitmap blocks via BlockManager
InodeManager calls BlockAllocator to obtain a free block when creating an inode
alloc_inode wraps InodeManager; read_file/write_file traverse inode block pointers managed by InodeManager
read_directory and directory write paths call read_file/write_file to access directory content stored as file data
DataServer's BlockAllocator is built on BlockManager; all block-level I/O goes through it
DataServer constructs a BlockAllocator directly to manage its local block pool and version table
MetadataServer reuses the single-node inode/FS layer (InodeManager, alloc_inode, directory ops) to manage distributed metadata
MetadataServer tests create real DataServer instances; allocate_block and free_block on the metadata server issue RPCs to data servers requiring DataServer to be implemented first
Client delegates all metadata operations (mknode, lookup, get_block_map, etc.) via RPC to the metadata server
Client issues read_data/write_data RPCs directly to data servers using the block map returned by the metadata server
Log replication extends AppendEntries introduced for heartbeats; a stable leader election is required before log entries can be replicated
Persistence adds durability to the replicated log; the in-memory log structure to persist is defined by the replication layer
RaftLog<Command> uses a BlockManager instance to write and read log entries and Raft metadata from disk
Snapshot compacts the persistent log; all log-index arithmetic introduced in persistence must be adapted for the snapshot offset
